title: Inside the Longevity Boom: Where Science Ends and Fantasy Begins
description: The longevity sector attracted $8.49B in 2024 (a 220% jump) and is projected to reach $8T by 2030 per UBS, as USC researcher Valter Longo warns people are living longer but sicker.

title: NIA ITP March 2026 Results: Zero Compounds Extended Lifespan
description: The NIH Interventions Testing Program released March 2026 results showing zero compounds extended lifespan; pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2% after 20 years of testing.

title: Senescence Consortium Targets Biomarker Gap
description: A new consortium launches to address the critical lack of validated senescence biomarkers, aiming to standardize measurement tools needed for clinical trials of senolytic therapies.

title: Atrogi Begins Human Trial for Muscle-Preserving Weight Loss Drug
description: Swedish biotech Atrogi initiates first-in-human trials for its muscle-preserving weight loss compound, addressing the major limitation of current GLP-1 therapies that cause lean mass loss.

title: Experimental Pill Cuts Bad Cholesterol by Up to 60%
description: A new oral cholesterol-lowering drug demonstrates up to 60% LDL reduction in early trials, potentially offering an alternative to injectable PCSK9 inhibitors for cardiovascular longevity.

title: LA Times: Longevity Industry Turns Aging into an Investable Market
description: Biotech, AI diagnostics, and venture capital are converging across Southern California to make longevity a scalable growth frontier, shifting from boutique wellness to investable healthtech.

title: Stop Treating Women as an Exception in Aging Science
description: Longevity.Technology calls for overhauling the default male-centric approach to aging research, arguing women's distinct biological aging trajectories require dedicated study protocols.

title: R1 Launches with $77.5M for Chronic Kidney Disease Drug
description: R1 Therapeutics emerges from stealth with $77.5M in funding to develop a novel chronic kidney disease treatment, targeting one of the most common age-related conditions.

title: Science Corp Lands $230M to Commercialize Vision Restoration Chip
description: Science Corp raises $230M to commercialize its bionic eye system for vision restoration, part of the growing healthspan-focused neurotechnology sector.

title: Gubra-AbbVie Obesity Drug Nears 10% Weight Loss Milestone
description: The Gubra-AbbVie obesity drug partnership approaches a key 10% weight loss efficacy threshold, adding to the growing pipeline of metabolic longevity therapeutics.

title: Sequential Banks $3.5M for Smarter Skin Health Platform
description: Sequential raises $3.5M to build an AI-driven skin health platform, reflecting growing investor interest in the skin longevity and regenerative beauty sector.

title: When Longevity Data Grows Up
description: Longevity.Technology shifts from observing to structuring the field, while UT Health San Antonio preps a $38M human trial testing three existing medicines for extending healthy lifespan.

title: Popular Anti-Aging Senolytic Combo D+Q Causes Brain Myelin Damage in Mice
description: Landmark PNAS study from the Crocker Lab finds dasatinib+quercetin (D+Q), a widely used senolytic combo, triggers myelin loss in the brain of both young and aged mice, raising safety concerns.

title: Avai Bio and Austrianova Begin Master Cell Bank for Klotho Anti-Aging Therapy
description: Avai Bio and Austrianova initiate GMP production of a master cell bank overexpressing alpha-Klotho, the 'longevity protein,' advancing their cell-based anti-aging therapy toward clinical use.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Hit $45.8B by 2033
description: The global longevity biotech market is valued at $12.5B in 2026 and projected to reach $45.8B by 2033 at a CAGR of 20.4%, driven by AI-optimized drug discovery pipelines.

title: Longevity Innovation Forum: Aging Drug v1 Targets Disease, v2 Will Target Aging Itself
description: At the Longevity Innovation Forum in San Diego, panelists discussed how first-gen aging drugs target age-related diseases while next-gen will target the aging process directly.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches $38M VITAL-H Trial Testing Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin
description: UT Health San Antonio preps one of the largest human longevity trials, testing rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in 700+ adults aged 60-65, backed by $38M from ARPA-H.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Steve Horvath highlights March 2026 as a strong month for everyday supplement research, noting new findings on Vitamin C's role in inhibiting ferroptosis-related aging pathways in primates.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles leading anti-aging companies including Altos Labs ($3B), Cambrian Bio ($30.8M ARPA-H award), Retro Biosciences, and others advancing epigenetic reprogramming and gene therapy.

title: Centrum Silver Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by 4 Months in COSMOS Trial
description: The COSMOS randomized trial found daily Centrum Silver slowed all five epigenetic aging clocks by about 4 months over 2 years, with greatest benefits for those biologically older than their actual age.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Where the Science Still Looks Strong
description: A Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding ABT263 and ARV825 most effective. The field is narrowing toward more selective, biomarker-driven approaches.

title: US Government Funds $144M PROSPR Program for First Human Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H launches PROSPR, a $144M program for the first human clinical trials targeting aging directly, featuring rapamycin-based approaches and marking a paradigm shift in longevity medicine.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Targets $420B as Investors Eye Explosive Growth
description: The global anti-aging therapy market is targeting a $420B milestone as next-gen cellular therapies and regenerative medicine drive exponential expansion beyond supplements and skincare.

title: Scientists Reveal Why a Popular Anti-Aging Compound May Also Fuel Cancer
description: Polyamines, celebrated for boosting cellular cleanup and healthy aging, are found to also turbocharge cancer growth via eIF5A2, revealing a dual role for these longevity molecules.

title: Brown University Wins $22M Federal Award to Study Anti-Aging Treatment
description: Brown and Rochester researchers will test whether an HIV drug can quiet chronic DNA-triggered inflammation to slow human aging, in the first major trial targeting the 'dark genome' mechanism.
